title: Travel podcast episode
explanation:
Travel podcast episode

Listen to part of a travel podcast.

Transcript

Presenter: This week we are talking about city breaks on a budget. Our guest, Martin Shaw, has visited more than thirty European cities without spending much money. He recommends travelling in the off-season, staying in hostels and booking local transport in advance. He also says that eating where local people eat is often cheaper and better than going to tourist restaurants.

questions.text: Martin has visited more than [*thirty|thirteen|forty-five] European cities.
questions.feedback: The presenter says more than thirty.

questions.text: He recommends travelling in the [*off-season|high season|winter only].
Local restaurants are often [*cheaper and better|more dangerous|closed early].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The episode is about city breaks on a ____.  | *budget | platform | runway
Martin suggests booking local transport in ____.  | *advance | secret | winter
Tourist restaurants are often more ____.  | *expensive | friendly | empty
The speaker is a travel ____.  | *presenter | judge | customer
The podcast gives [*practical advice|medical warnings|legal information].
The focus is on travelling [*cheaply|luxuriously|for business only].

questions.feedback: Off-season, hostels, local transport, local restaurants.
